frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: sensory codes optimized to represent natural stimuli given downstream cost.

frameworks.name: rate-distortion
frameworks.one_line: Lp reconstruction error of ML decoder as parametric distortion family.

frameworks.name: information maximization (infomax)
frameworks.one_line: recovered as a special case of the Lp criterion.

frameworks.name: Bayesian observer
frameworks.one_line: maximum likelihood decoder supplies the reconstruction used in the loss.

core_question: Which optimality criterion best explains tuning-curve shape in early sensory neural codes?
key_assumptions: single neuron encodes a one-dimensional stimulus with arbitrary input distribution
downstream readout is the maximum likelihood decoder
encoder optimality is defined by Lp reconstruction error
population codes can be summarized by a meta-tuning curve
manipulations: exponent p of the Lp reconstruction loss
key_findings: analytic optimal tuning curve derived for a single neuron under arbitrary input distribution
meta-tuning-curve construction generalizes the result to a class of neural populations
Lp family contains infomax and squared-error criteria as special cases
biological early-visual tuning data are best matched by low values of p, close to the infomax limit
interpretations: early visual systems across animals optimize similar reconstruction-style criteria near information maximization
Lp reconstruction error unifies infomax and decoding-error views of efficient coding
